mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate exceeding a 0.3 percentage point monthly increase signals an impending recessionary shock, triggering risk-off deleveraging and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and housing starts indicates a structural demand mismatch where consumption is decoupling from investment, leading to earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows a positive correlation with forward returns in walk-forward tests, or the combined signal fails to outperform a baseline GDPC1-only model in out-of-sample Sharpe ratios.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a sustained industrial production index below its pre-crisis trend level indicates persistent capacity constraints that suppress corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Industrial production rebounds above the threshold without triggering equity drawdowns, or the signal yields a negative Rank IC during the specified recovery window.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real disposable income (proxied by RSAFS) directly boosts consumer spending capacity, driving near-term earnings momentum and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in more than 60% of instances where RSAFS rises, or the signal exhibits zero correlation with equity prices during high-volatility episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 0.25 percent, it signals the terminal phase of the zero-lower-bound policy, compressing equity risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally persistently after the rate threshold is breached, or the forward return differential between pre- and post-threshold periods is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which historically precedes broad-based earnings upgrades and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in the majority of historical instances, or the interaction term fails to add incremental predictive power over PAYEMS alone in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: In a stagflationary environment, a month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.5 percent erodes real purchasing power and forces aggressive monetary tightening expectations, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Equity prices remain stable or rise despite the inflation spike, or the signal generates a positive Rank IC during high-inflation periods, contradicting the stagflation premise.
